name: Agent4Edu Compares Generated Learner Responses Against Human Learner Records
statement: Agent4Edu evaluates generated learner responses against real EduData learner response records, making human-response consistency a central quality signal for the simulator.
population_or_context: Chinese high school mathematics and physics personalized learning response records.
quantitative_note: The staged extraction records 18,045 response records from 500 students and reports Agent4Edu (GPT-3.5-turbo) at 66.70 ACC, 79.84 F1-score, and 37.97 ROUGE-3.
caveats: The evaluation measures simulator fidelity rather than scorer validity.
The generated responses are initialized from real learner histories, so this is not an independent real-vs-synthetic held-out comparison.
evaluation_method: Consistency and discrepancy assessment between generated agent responses and human learner responses.
reported_metrics: ACC, F1-score, and ROUGE-3 for learner simulation.
limitations: The response setting is personalized learning practice data, not rubric-scored constructed responses.
The source does not establish synthetic responses as validation data for an automated scorer.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases the real learner-response dataset, simulator-response comparison, and Table 1 metrics.
